name: Tonelli-Shanks square root mod prime p

variants.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_tonelli && printf '8\n0 13\n10 13\n2 13\n2 23\n3 23\n4 17\n3 17\n4 1000000007\n' > /tmp/eval_tonelli/input.txt && cat > /tmp/eval_tonelli/check.py <<'PYEOF'
inp = open('input.txt').read().split()
out = open('out.txt').read().splitlines()
T = int(inp[0]); idx = 1
for t in range(T):
    n, p = int(inp[idx]), int(inp[idx+1]); idx += 2
    line = out[t].strip()
    is_qr = (n == 0) or pow(n, (p-1)//2, p) == 1
    if not is_qr:
        assert line == 'NONE', f'case {t}: n={n} p={p} expected NONE got {line!r}'
    else:
        r = int(line)
        assert 0 <= r < p, f'case {t}: r={r} out of range'
        assert (r * r) % p == n % p, f'case {t}: r^2 mod p = {(r*r)%p} != n={n%p}'
print('OK')
PYEOF
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_tonelli/ts.py — reads from stdin: line 1 is T, next T lines are 'n p' (p is an odd prime, 0 <= n < p). For each, output an integer r in [0, p) with r*r ≡ n (mod p), or 'NONE' (uppercase) if no such r exists. Either of the two roots is acceptable.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_tonelli && python3 ts.py < input.txt > out.txt && python3 check.py

variants.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_tonelli && printf '8\n0 13\n10 13\n2 13\n2 23\n3 23\n4 17\n3 17\n4 1000000007\n' > /tmp/eval_tonelli/input.txt && cat > /tmp/eval_tonelli/check.py <<'PYEOF'
inp = open('input.txt').read().split()
out = open('out.txt').read().splitlines()
T = int(inp[0]); idx = 1
for t in range(T):
    n, p = int(inp[idx]), int(inp[idx+1]); idx += 2
    line = out[t].strip()
    is_qr = (n == 0) or pow(n, (p-1)//2, p) == 1
    if not is_qr:
        assert line == 'NONE', f'case {t}: expected NONE got {line!r}'
    else:
        r = int(line)
        assert (r * r) % p == n % p, f'case {t}: r^2 != n'
print('OK')
PYEOF
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_tonelli/ts.go (package main). Reads stdin: T, then T lines 'n p'. Output r (0..p-1) with r*r ≡ n (mod p), or 'NONE' if no root exists. Use math/big for the modular exponentiation in the witness search to handle large primes. Compile with `go build -o ts ts.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_tonelli && go build -o ts ts.go && ./ts < input.txt > out.txt && python3 check.py

out = open('out.txt').read().splitlines()
T = int(inp[0]); idx = 1
for t in range(T):
    n, p = int(inp[idx]), int(inp[idx+1]); idx += 2
    line = out[t].strip()
    is_qr = (n == 0) or pow(n, (p-1)//2, p) == 1
    if not is_qr:
        assert line == 'NONE', f'case {t}: expected NONE got {line!r}'
    else:
        r = int(line)
        assert (r * r) % p == n % p, f'case {t}: r^2 != n'
print('OK')
PYEOF
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_tonelli/ts.c — reads stdin: T, then T lines 'n p' (uint64_t). Output r or 'NONE'. Use __int128 for modular multiplication so n*n doesn't overflow. Must compile cleanly with `gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o ts ts.c`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_tonelli && gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o ts ts.c && ./ts < input.txt > out.txt && python3 check.py
